kernel-hardening.name: Kernel Hardening
kernel-hardening.description: OS kernel-level security controls that harden memory layout, stack/heap protections, and kernel self-protection mechanisms.

network-isolation.name: Network Isolation
network-isolation.description: Controls that segment, filter, and restrict network traffic to limit lateral movement and reduce attack surface.

mandatory-access-control.name: Mandatory Access Control
mandatory-access-control.description: Kernel-enforced access control frameworks that confine processes to least-privilege regardless of DAC permissions.

cryptographic-controls.name: Cryptographic Controls
cryptographic-controls.description: Controls that protect data confidentiality and integrity through cryptographic mechanisms for data in transit and at rest.

filesystem-hardening.name: Filesystem Hardening
filesystem-hardening.description: Controls that restrict filesystem access, enforce mount options, and protect file integrity.

syscall-bpf-controls.name: Syscall & BPF Controls
syscall-bpf-controls.description: Controls that filter, restrict, or monitor system call usage and eBPF program loading.

container-isolation.name: Container Isolation
container-isolation.description: Controls that strengthen container runtime boundaries, sandbox untrusted workloads, and enforce pod-level security policies.

privilege-isolation.name: Privilege Isolation
privilege-isolation.description: Controls that separate privilege domains, enforce least-privilege, and restrict privilege escalation paths.

credential-hardening.name: Credential Hardening
credential-hardening.description: Controls that strengthen authentication mechanisms, enforce password policies, and protect credential storage.

protocol-hardening.name: Protocol Hardening
protocol-hardening.description: Controls that harden network protocol configurations, disable insecure protocol versions, and reduce protocol-level attack surface.
expected_scope: SNMP version and community string hardening

application-controls.name: Application Controls
application-controls.description: Application-layer security controls including secure headers, CORS policy, dependency management, and runtime application security.

runtime-detection.name: Runtime Detection
runtime-detection.description: Controls that detect security threats, anomalies, and policy violations at runtime through monitoring, logging, and analysis.

integrity-detection.name: Integrity Detection
integrity-detection.description: Controls that verify the integrity of system components, configurations, and software to detect unauthorized modifications.

patch-management.name: Patch Management
patch-management.description: Controls that ensure timely application of security patches, automated update mechanisms, and vulnerability remediation processes.

recovery-controls.name: Recovery Controls
recovery-controls.description: Controls that enable system recovery after security incidents through backups, snapshots, and disaster recovery mechanisms.

application-input-validation.name: Application Input Validation
application-input-validation.description: Controls that validate, sanitize, and constrain application inputs to prevent injection attacks and input-driven vulnerabilities.
